The Bank of China has announced that, effective from the close of settlement on February 11, 2026:
For Gold Deferred Contracts:
• The Shanghai Gold Exchange (SGE) margin requirement will be adjusted from 18% to 21%.
• The Bank's client margin requirement will be adjusted from 47.52% to 55.44%.
• Starting from the next trading day, the SGE price fluctuation limit (trading band) will be adjusted from 17% to 20%.

• The Shanghai Gold Exchange (SGE) margin requirement will be adjusted from 24% to 27%.
• The Bank's client margin requirement will be adjusted from 68.88% to 77.49%.
• Starting from the next trading day, the SGE price fluctuation limit (trading band) will be adjusted from 23% to 26%.



Silver prices are likely to fluctuate significantly this week as well.

investors are not trading. trading is speculating.
the key difference is that investors buy shares in order to hold a portion of a company that products anything. they don't really care about price... of course, they also like discounts. I know, bcs i made my wealth out of investing:
while traders are only speculating on price actions.. buying shares only to sell them for a bettern price, and vice versa.
this elevates volatility, but most importantly: misvaluation is spreading like skibidi toilett.

what i said is that investors don't like these high prices anymore and wait for discounts... also some of them scaling out of companies, bcs they are uncertain about companies how will they survive the next 4 year window, and ready to buy back for lower prices, owning bigger portion of the company if it worths it.
this is why i keep telling that this is trader's market... Soros, Cohen, Dimon and other ppl like them... they are the ones ruling the market now

comment.body:
so what i want to say is that there is a huge difference between what quants on net say, and what big funds actually use.
i know fund managers. big ones. very big ones. i also consulted some of them, and others offered me jobs.

what they do are 
1) investing. funds are mostly long. they make a tons of qualitativ researches on companies: moneyflow, performance, leadership, demand-side, marketing... also the bigger ones can use intelligence infos on ceos, concurences etc. to judge if they wanna buy into the party. If the valuation is too high, they wait for a discount, but usually for investing you don't care about the market, bcs your income does not come from the stock spot price.
2) on the short-run they make speculative trades. mostly short realized volatility strategies, statistical arbitrage strats, market making strats. Why? bcs of the positive EV. I made post on that, and will do in the future.
3) also momentum trading, when the vol environment leans towards that. like now. simple long/short future tradings at support and resistance levels just like anybody else...
the levels are derived from many datas: positioning, rough vol based estimations, economic datas... other sectors' flows etc. 
here they also monitor their 'partner' and their 'competitors', because when you trade with billions, you need to be careful... also you must size your positions carefully in order to avoid your own effect on the spot, except if thats your plan against your competitor. 

so what these quants do is not simply "running machine learning, deep learning etc. based pattern recognitions and sizing algos" on spot price and boom , they predict them...
but 1) they carefully monitor there concurences and go into war against them.
2) they buy datas you can't even imagine aside of intelligence datas, soc media datas, deep/dark web datas, psychological profilling AIs (to be one step ahead of the ceos companies, politicians, other funds), aside of economic datas like shipping, air traffic, actual economic data reports (not the ones you see published) etc-etc.
